subject: science
title: Grade 2 Science
description: States of matter, animal habitats, and pushes and pulls.

lessons.title: States of Matter
lessons.objective: Identify solids, liquids, and gases and their properties.
lessons.content:
Everything around us is made of MATTER, and matter exists in three states:

🧊 SOLID — has a fixed shape and size
  Examples: ice, wood, rock, books
  Can you hold it? It keeps its shape!

💧 LIQUID — flows and takes the shape of its container
  Examples: water, milk, juice, oil
  Pour it into a cup — it fills the cup's shape!

💨 GAS — spreads out to fill all available space
  Examples: air, steam, helium in balloons
  You can't usually see it, but it's there!

Water is special — it can be all three:
  Ice (solid) → Water (liquid) → Steam (gas)

exercises.question: Which state of matter takes the shape of its container?
exercises.hint: Think about pouring juice into different cups.
exercises.explanation: Liquids flow and take the shape of their container. Pour water into a glass and it becomes glass-shaped!

exercises.question: What happens to water when it gets very cold?
exercises.hint: What happens when you put water in a freezer?
exercises.explanation: When water gets very cold (below 0°C), it freezes and becomes ice, which is a solid!

lessons.title: Animal Habitats
lessons.objective: Understand what a habitat is and match animals to their habitats.
lessons.content:
A HABITAT is the place where an animal lives. It provides:
  • Food to eat
  • Water to drink
  • Shelter for safety
  • Space to move around

🌊 OCEAN — whales, dolphins, fish, sea turtles
🌳 FOREST — deer, squirrels, owls, bears
🏜️ DESERT — camels, lizards, scorpions, snakes
❄️ ARCTIC — polar bears, penguins, seals, arctic foxes
🌿 POND — frogs, ducks, dragonflies, newts

Animals have ADAPTATIONS that help them survive:
  • Polar bears have thick fur (stays warm)
  • Camels store water (survives in desert)
  • Fish have gills (breathe underwater)

exercises.question: Which animal would you most likely find in an arctic habitat?
exercises.hint: Which animal has thick white fur for cold places?
exercises.explanation: Polar bears live in the Arctic! They have thick fur and a layer of fat to stay warm in freezing temperatures.

exercises.question: A habitat provides food, water, shelter, and ______ for animals. (one word)
exercises.hint: Animals need room to move, hunt, and explore.
exercises.explanation: A habitat provides food, water, shelter, and SPACE for animals to live and move around.
